summary: Do not ship yet. `Replace` closes the declarative legacy-Job hole, but P-2 still lacks the pre-landing transition test required by r6. No new critical issue or materially simpler safe alternative was found.

findings.title: Open question: which seam tests active legacy Job replacement?
findings.body: `Replace` matches [Kubernetes concurrency semantics](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/cron-jobs/#concurrency-policy), but L7 only injects a synthetic heartbeat, preflight only checks YAML values, and the post-landing observation does not guarantee an active pre-change Job. Every planned gate can therefore pass without exercising deletion of the unbounded legacy Job, creation of its bounded replacement, and heartbeat delivery before `for:`—the exact transition that caused r6 to reject P-2.
findings.recommendation: Add a required transition test against the pinned Kubernetes version: seed a hanging pre-change `Forbid` Job, apply `Replace` plus `activeDeadlineSeconds`, then assert the old Job UID is deleted, a bounded successor starts, and its heartbeat arrives before 900 seconds.

next_steps: Add the active-legacy-Job transition test and rerun the focused P-2 review.
